title: The Benefits of Community Gardens
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Shared (0) communal gardens are springing up in cities around the world, transforming unused land into vibrant green spaces. These projects offer a wide range of (17) environmental benefits, such as improving air quality and reducing the urban heat island effect. Growing fruit and vegetables (18) locally also reduces the need to transport food long distances, lowering carbon emissions.||Community gardens are equally (19) valuable for social wellbeing. They bring together people of all ages, creating a sense of (20) belonging and shared purpose. Participants report that regular gardening can be deeply (21) therapeutic and helps to combat feelings of (22) loneliness. Working alongside others also promotes a spirit of (23) cooperation and improves (24) interpersonal relationships.

example.text: Shared (0) COMMUNAL gardens are springing up in cities around the world, transforming unused land into vibrant green spaces. (COMMUNITY)

questions.explanation: The adjective 'environmental' is formed from 'environment' by adding the suffix '-al'.
The adverb 'locally' is formed from 'local' by adding the suffix '-ly'.
The adjective 'valuable' is formed from 'value' by replacing '-ue' with '-uable'.
The gerund/noun 'belonging' is formed from 'belong' by adding the suffix '-ing'.
The adjective 'therapeutic' is formed from 'therapy' by replacing '-y' with '-eutic'.
The noun 'loneliness' is formed from 'lonely' by changing '-y' to '-iness'.
The noun 'cooperation' is formed from 'cooperate' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
The adjective 'interpersonal' uses the prefix 'inter-' and suffix '-al' combined with 'person'.
